task: Write a Python function truncate_words(s, n) returning the first n whitespace-separated words of the string joined by single spaces, so runs of whitespace collapse. When n is zero or negative return ''. When the string has fewer than n words return them all. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all criteria with no edge cases unhandled.
trace:
**
I defined the function `truncate_words` that takes a string `s` and an integer `n`. If `n` is zero or negative, the function returns an empty string. Otherwise, it splits the input string into words, selects the first `n` words, and joins them back together with single spaces. This approach ensures that runs of whitespace are collapsed into a single space, as required.
